original_hypothesis.title: ManagedNFTs Array Modification During Partial Withdrawal Corrupts nextWithdrawal Index
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: withdrawFromManagedNFTs uses a stateful cursor (nextWithdrawal) to track progress through partial withdrawal batches. If the owner calls releaseManagedNFT or addManagedNFT while nextWithdrawal > 0, the swap-and-pop array modification can cause: (1) the NFT swapped to the removed index to be skipped (its revenue uncollected), (2) if enough NFTs are removed, nextWithdrawal > ManagedNFTs.length, meaning the reset condition `nextWithdrawal == ManagedNFTs.length` never triggers, permanently stalling the withdrawal cycle.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: releaseManagedNFT and addManagedNFT have no guard for nextWithdrawal > 0. The swap-and-pop in releaseManagedNFT changes array ordering that the withdrawal cursor depends on. If nextWithdrawal exceeds the new array length, the loop in withdrawFromManagedNFTs starts at an index beyond the array, processes nothing, and cannot reset.

proof_of_signal.pattern: nextWithdrawal cursor not protected from ManagedNFTs array modifications; swap-and-pop in releaseManagedNFT can cause nextWithdrawal > ManagedNFTs.length

preconditions: Owner calls withdrawFromManagedNFTs with partial batch (nextWithdrawal > 0)
Owner then calls releaseManagedNFT before withdrawal cycle completes
Released NFT is at index < nextWithdrawal

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: nextWithdrawal stays at old value while array shrinks; if nextWithdrawal >= new length, reset condition never met
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin releasing NFTs during partial withdrawal permanently stalls withdrawal cycle
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit — prevents revenue collection from all remaining managed NFTs

economic_questions: Can the withdrawal cycle recover if more NFTs are added to increase array length?
What is the exact scenario where nextWithdrawal exceeds array length?
Is there any way to manually reset nextWithdrawal?
validation_requirements: Test partial withdrawal followed by releaseManagedNFT at various indices
Verify stall condition when nextWithdrawal > ManagedNFTs.length
Check if addManagedNFT can unstall by growing the array

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Revenue collection delayed or permanently blocked

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Index arithmetic for various release scenarios

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
